behavior_in_task_description.reason: All asserted names (event_buffer, mock_client, upload/stream, download_file/upload_file/drop_file, TEST_* fixtures, expect_download/set_file_inputs/drop_files, ./download-command export, README references) are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what to create and required names/APIs, but doesn't dictate line-level implementation; developer must still figure out how to implement.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert literal strings like 'export async function download_file' (test_download_module_functions), 'export const TEST_TXT' (test_fixtures_module), and 'event_buffer.push' — a valid alternative implementation using e.g. a class method, default-exported function, or different pushing mechanism would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most f2p tests are grep-style text checks (e.g., test_render_event_buffer, test_listen_retrospective_mode, test_mock_client_function, test_download_module_functions, test_fixtures_module, test_vite_config_browser_commands, README tests) that only inspect file contents without executing the code.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require specific identifiers and exports that a no-op stub couldn't satisfy without at least declaring them; repo build/format/type tests require the code to compile.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each required behavior in instruction.md (fixtures, download utilities, browser commands, event buffering, mock_client, package exports, README content) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates pass_to_pass, Fail-to-pass pr_diff, Config/documentation) and each has a descriptive docstring and tag.

structured_data_schema.reason: The package.json exports schema ('./download-command' key) is explicitly documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names (js/tootils/src/..., @gradio/tootils, expect_download, etc.) are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files to create/modify, which is inherent to the task (creating new utilities), not leakage of buggy-line localization.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Config edits (package.json export, vite.config.ts command registration) are narrowly scoped additions, not full-file rewrites, and don't conflict with other files.
